module_map.responsibility: AST node definitions for all SQL constructs. Hierarchical classes: Expression → Func → specific functions (e.g., Levenshtein, Soundex). Organized by domain: string.py, math.py, aggregate.py, temporal.py, query.py, dml.py.
Token definitions and Tokenizer class. Wraps _TokenizerCore (external Rust engine via sqlglotrs). Manages dialect-specific token mappings and quoting rules.
SQL parser: consumes Token stream from Tokenizer, produces Expression AST tree. Handles dialect-specific parsing via function_parsers dict. 425+ functions, 9508 lines.
SQL code generator: converts Expression tree back to SQL string. Dialect-specific generation via generate_* methods. 454+ functions, 5744 lines.

Dialect-specific implementations. Each dialect (e.g., bigquery.py, postgres.py) extends Dialect, Parser, and Generator. Registers function name mappings (e.g., EDITDISTANCE to Levenshtein). Central hub: dialect.py (91,948 bytes).
AST optimization passes: scope analysis, type annotation, predicate pushdown, join elimination, etc. Operates on Expression trees. 15+ optimizer modules.
Python-based SQL execution engine. Interprets Expression trees and executes operations. Minimal use in core pipeline; optional feature.

pipeline_trace.description: SQL string split into tokens using dialect-specific rules. Calls sqlglotrs (Rust backend) via _TokenizerCore.
types_produced: Token (contains type: TokenType, text: str, line: int, col: int)

pipeline_trace.description: Token stream consumed via recursive descent parsing. Dialect-specific function_parsers dict maps SQL func names (e.g., LEVENSHTEIN) to parser builders. Returns Expression tree.
Parser creates typed Expression nodes. Example: Levenshtein(this=Identifier(col1), expression=Identifier(col2)) with optional max_dist arg.

pipeline_trace.key_function: optimize(ast: Expression, schema: Schema) returns Expression
pipeline_trace.description: AST transformation passes. Type annotation, scope binding, predicate pushdown. Mutates Expression tree in place.

Expression tree traversed, generate_*() methods called per node type. Dialect subclasses override to emit dialect-specific SQL. Example: Levenshtein produces LEVENSHTEIN(...), EDITDISTANCE(...), or LEVENSHTEIN_DISTANCE(...).

cross_module_hubs.reason: Central registry. Every dialect imports Dialect base class. All parsers/generators inherit from its Parser/Generator. dialect.py imports from: expressions, parser, generator, tokens, errors, helper, jsonpath. Acts as dependency aggregator and factory (get_or_raise).

Core AST vocabulary. Imported by parser.py, generator.py, all dialects, all optimizers, executor. All language constructs (SELECT, Levenshtein, etc.) defined here. Minimal outbound: only core.py imports typing, helper.
Pipeline entry point. Imported by: dialects/dialect.py, parsers/* (specializations), __init__.py (public API), tests. Outbound: tokens, expressions, errors, helper, trie, time.

change_proposal.objective: Add LEVENSHTEIN(str1, str2) as a canonical scalar function
new_types: Levenshtein (already exists, line 74 in string.py) with optional ins_cost, del_cost, sub_cost, max_dist
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Levenshtein is the canonical type. Pattern: (1) Expression class in expressions/string.py with arg_types dict. (2) Parser function in parsers/base.py (function_parsers dict) maps SQL name to Expression builder. (3) Dialect specializations override parser (parsers/*) or generator (dialects/*) for dialect-specific syntax.

implementation_steps.details: Add to Parser.FUNCTIONS dict: LEVENSHTEIN: exp.Levenshtein.from_arg_list (already in parsers/clickhouse.py, parsers/presto.py). Makes LEVENSHTEIN(str1, str2) parse to Levenshtein node.
Ensure levenshtein_sql() method exists and is called for Levenshtein nodes. Default: LEVENSHTEIN(' + args + ')'.

implementation_steps.step: 3. Add dialect overrides for non-standard naming
implementation_steps.details: dialects/snowflake.py: rename_func(EDITDISTANCE). dialects/presto.py: rename_func(LEVENSHTEIN_DISTANCE). dialects/clickhouse.py: already handles EDITDISTANCE.
tests/test_build.py: add test parsing SELECT LEVENSHTEIN(a, b) across dialects. Verify transpilation: postgres to clickhouse changes output correctly.

change_proposal.integration_point: Levenshtein already in expression type system (expressions/string.py). Gaps: (1) Base parser may lack LEVENSHTEIN in function_parsers. (2) Generator may lack levenshtein_sql() or use fallback func_sql().
risks: Risk 1: Name collision. LEVENSHTEIN is non-standard. Postgres: LEVENSHTEIN, ClickHouse: EDITDISTANCE, Presto: LEVENSHTEIN_DISTANCE. Solution: Levenshtein class supports via _sql_names or dialect TRANSFORMS.
Risk 2: Missing optional args (ins_cost, del_cost, sub_cost, max_dist). Solution: arg_types marks them False (optional); generator skips if None.
Risk 3: Generator fallback. If levenshtein_sql() missing, falls back to func_sql() outputting generic LEVENSHTEIN(...). Verify transpilation tests pass.

Risk 4: Parser registration conflicts. Different dialects may have conflicting parsers. Solution: dialects/dialect.py merges rules; last wins. Ensure base parsers/base.py LEVENSHTEIN doesn't conflict.
migration_checklist: Verify exp.Levenshtein exists with arg_types (found at line 74-82 in expressions/string.py)
Check parsers/base.py for LEVENSHTEIN in Parser.FUNCTIONS
Test: sqlglot.parse_one(SELECT LEVENSHTEIN(a, b)) returns Levenshtein node
Test: transpile across dialects (postgres to clickhouse) matches dialect syntax

tool_usage.findings: Top-level modules: sqlglot (core), dialects/, optimizer/, executor/. 64 files, 50888 LOC.
Tokenizer wraps _TokenizerCore (Rust). Methods: tokenize(sql: str). 2 classes, 8 functions, 587 LOC.
parse() in 6 files. Main: parser.py:1887 Parser.parse(). Public API: __init__.py:88.
Class at expressions/string.py:74. Used by: parsers/clickhouse.py, parsers/snowflake.py, parsers/postgres.py, parsers/drill.py, parsers/presto.py.
110 files import from sqlglot. Top: expressions, Dialect, Parser, Generator, tokens. Confirms hub pattern.

tool_usage.params: Wildcard imports from submodules. ALL_FUNCTIONS registry via subclasses(). FUNCTION_BY_NAME built dynamically.

tool_usage.findings: arg_types: this (required), expression, ins_cost, del_cost, sub_cost, max_dist (optional).
